meaning: n.漂白细软薄布,混乱vt.研磨,深思vi.温热
examples: I need some time to mull it over before making a decision.
Now we shall be able to mull some ale!
But who has time to mull over things of this nature?
Launches from Tobermory on Mull jumped from just two last summer to 15 this year.
Publishers are only just beginning to mull over the potential uses for e-reading data.
